name: Kuaokala Ridge Trail
tagline: A remote Waianae ridge trail with views that rival anything on the island — and almost no one there.
parking: Free parking at the Kuaokala Game Management Area access road off Farrington Highway — the gate is sometimes locked, check DLNR for access
hours: Open sunrise to sunset; access gate may be locked weekdays — check Hawaii DLNR schedule
bestTime: Weekend mornings when the access gate is reliably open
talkStory: About 5 miles round trip along the Waianae Range with 1,500 ft elevation gain. The trail runs along the spine of the ridge with jaw-dropping views of Makua Valley on one side and the ocean on the other. This area is part of an Army/DLNR managed zone, so access can be limited — call ahead or check the DLNR website. When it's open, it's one of the most stunning ridge walks on Oahu. The trail connects to Kealia Trail for a much longer through-hike. Bring wind protection — it's exposed and breezy at the top.
